fields.description:
Harden `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` against disk exhaustion from repeated failed rebuilds (incident 2731: 41h of per-main-advance rebuild attempts accumulated multi-GB buildkit cache + dangling layers on the 30G root disk until pip/clone ENOSPC fail-closed the LLM-Review gate). Today `docker image prune -f` runs ONLY on the success path (line 166) and nothing ever prunes the buildkit build cache on either path — a failing deploy loop is exactly when garbage accumulates fastest and is exactly the path with no reclamation.

## What (pinned design — no alternatives left to the executor)
- Add one helper `prune_docker_caches()` in autodeploy.sh that runs, best-effort:
  1. `docker builder prune -f --keep-storage "${BUILD_CACHE_KEEP:-5GB}"` — a HARD storage cap on the buildkit cache (quantified bound; `--filter until=…` was rejected as time-based and unbounded under a tight failure loop). `BUILD_CACHE_KEEP` joins the existing tunables block (overridable via /etc/rebar/autodeploy.env).
  2. `docker image prune -f` — dangling images from failed/superseded builds.
  Both invocations discard stdout and `|| true`: a prune failure must NEVER mask the deploy-failure exit code or alter control flow (same idiom as the existing success-path prune). But it is not fully silent: on a prune non-zero exit the helper emits one `log "prune_docker_caches: ... failed (non-fatal)"` journald line, so a persistently failing prune is visible in the unit journal rather than invisibly leaving the disk-pressure defect live.
- Flag verification (DONE, pre-plan): probed the deploy box via SSM on 2026-07-10 — `docker builder prune --help` on Docker 25.0.14 (build 0bab007) documents `--keep-storage bytes   Amount of disk space to keep for cache`. The flag is confirmed on the exact engine that runs this script.
- Each prune invocation is wrapped in `timeout 120` (a slow/wedged daemon under the very disk pressure this targets must not hold the deploy lock indefinitely; 120s is generous for a prune).
- Call `prune_docker_caches` from `record_backoff_failure()` — the single seam every failure exit already goes through (build fail, health-check rollback, checkout/rsync fail) — so every failed attempt reclaims before backing off.
- Replace the success path's bare `docker image prune -f` with the same helper, so the buildkit cache is also capped on success (the incident's cache accumulated across BOTH outcomes; keeping ≤5GB warm cache preserves rebuild speed).
- Note: on the health-check-rollback path the freshly built image is TAGGED (`:latest` re-tagged from `:prev` after rollback leaves the bad build's layers referenced by nothing after the next successful build); the `--keep-storage` builder cap plus dangling-image prune is the bounded, safe reclamation — we deliberately do NOT delete tagged images (`:prev` is the rollback lifeline).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1 (failure path prunes): with `docker` stubbed by a PATH-front shim that logs argv and exits per-scenario, driving `record_backoff_failure` records exactly one `builder prune -f --keep-storage 5GB` and one `image prune -f` invocation, and the function still writes the backoff file with the original fail count. Harness: a NEW CI-run pytest module `tests/unit/test_autodeploy_prune.py` that sources the helper functions from autodeploy.sh in a bash subprocess with a tmp-dir PATH-front `docker` (and `timeout`) shim logging argv; invocation: `pytest tests/unit/test_autodeploy_prune.py` (runs in `make test`, so CI itself is the proof).
- [ ] AC2 (prune failure is inert): with the stub forcing both prune commands to exit 1, `record_backoff_failure` still records the backoff and the helper returns 0 (prune can never mask the deploy failure), AND the helper emits the `prune_docker_caches: … failed (non-fatal)` log line (asserted on captured output) — same pytest module as AC1.
- [ ] AC3 (bounded growth, quantified): the buildkit cache bound is the `--keep-storage` value (default 5GB, tunable via `BUILD_CACHE_KEEP`); proving commands (both must pass): `grep -q 'docker builder prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` (the capped prune exists) AND `! grep 'docker builder prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh | grep -qv 'keep-storage'` (no uncapped builder prune exists) — asserted inside the AC1 pytest module so an absent call cannot false-pass.
- [ ] AC4 (syntax + config-check): `bash -n infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` passes and `make config-check` stays green (check 4 covers script syntax).
- [ ] AC5 (success path uses the helper): the success path calls `prune_docker_caches` and contains no remaining bare `docker image prune` outside the helper; proving command: `grep -c 'docker image prune' infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` returns 1 (the helper's single occurrence) and the AC1 stub harness driven through the success path records the same two prune invocations.
